question: A mixed-spin Ising model is defined on a two-dimensional square lattice with Hamiltonian H = J1 * sum_{<i,j>} sigma_i * S_j + D * sum_{j in B} S_j^2 - J2 * sum_{<<i,j>> in B} S_i * S_j, where J1>0 is the antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor coupling between A-sublattice spins sigma_i=±1 and B-sublattice spins S_j=0,±1, D is the single-site anisotropy on B, and J2 is the next-nearest-neighbor coupling among B spins. Monte Carlo simulations are used to study the phase transitions in the antiferromagnetic (AF), the 0I (half-B-spins in state S=0), and the B-AF phases. What is the ORDER and UNIVERSALITY CLASS of the finite-temperature phase transitions in the AF and 0I phases of this model? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt", and "universality_class" using one of: "2D Ising", "3D Ising", "BKT", "other".

metadata.system: Mixed-spin Ising model (sigma=±1 and S=0,±1) on 2D square lattice with NNN B-B coupling
metadata.source_excerpt: For the AF (antiferromagnetic) phase, the 0I phase, and the B-AF phase, the finite-temperature transitions are in the two-dimensional Ising universality class (continuous second-order transitions with 2D Ising critical exponents)
metadata.difficulty_note: Model has compensation points (below Tc where sublattice magnetizations cancel) but the transition itself is standard 2D Ising universality class.

cold_solve.probe_model: claude-sonnet baseline (a cloud HPC platform sandbox for C1)
